acceptable_unverifiable.notes: Right artist confirmed: the albumDNA's biographical opening is taken near-verbatim from the entity-matched Abdullah Ibrahim Wikipedia article (Dollar Brand, Cape jazz, Monk/Ellington influence, 'Mannenberg' anti-apartheid anthem) — all consistent, no contradiction. However MB returned no release for this album, and the wiki page is the ARTIST bio (it does not mention 'African Marketplace' in the available extract). So the album's own substantive facts — year 1980, label Elektra Musician, and the 4 keyTracks (African Marketplace, Zimbabwe, The Wedding, Soweto) — cannot be checked against any surviving evidence. The album-specific editorial lines (introduced Ibrahim to American audiences; positive energy vs apartheid) are opinion. unverifiable: correct entity, no contradiction, but the album metadata/tracklist is unconfirmable. The evidence stored_url was the artist page (correct person) but ours.wikipediaUrl is null, so none_stored.

Entity solidly confirmed: MB embedded_mbid title/1965-06/Argo (Argo 749)/Baby-Face Willette, all 5 keyTracks match. Substantive claims that cannot be checked against the evidence: 'his final album', 'recorded in late 1964', 'Cut in Chicago'. No contradiction, but these are uncheckable. The stored evidence wiki URL is 'Face to Face (Baby Face Willette album)' — a DIFFERENT album (1961 Blue Note debut), wrong entity; ours.wikipediaUrl is null so none_stored. The DNA's reference to earlier Blue Note sessions is consistent with that page.

MB search (similarity 1.0): Dizzy Atmosphere, Dizzy Gillespie, Compilation, label Flashback Home Entertainment, no date. ours keyTracks (Hot House, Groovin' High, Dizzy Atmosphere, All the Things You Are, I Can't Get Started, Blue n' Boogie) ALL appear in this MB compilation tracklist -> ours describes the MB Gillespie bebop compilation. The Wikipedia 'Dizzy Atmosphere (album)' page is a DIFFERENT album (1957 Specialty LP led by members of Gillespie's orchestra incl. Lee Morgan, arr. Roger Spotts) -- not our entity; wikipedia entityMatch=no (no URL stored, so no shipped wrong link). DNA 'Recorded in 1997' is suspicious for a compilation of 1940s-era recordings, but MB release date is empty and gives no recording date to contradict it verbatim -> unverifiable, not factual_error. Year field 1997 also unconfirmable (MB has no date). Recording-year claim should be reviewed.

DNA entity-matched and accurate: 'Released in 1966 on Atlantic' matches MB (release date 1966, label Atlantic); track program The Drum Also Waltzes/Nommo/title track/For Big Sid matches MB tracklist exactly; keyTracks all match. label 'Atlantic' is correct. The only soft spot is the year field 2004, which disagrees with the matched MB release (1966) and the DNA's own '1966'. No evidence shows a 2004 release, but a 2004 reissue plausibly exists, so per 'absence is not contradiction' the year cannot be called a factual_error -- it is unverifiable. (If the catalog intends 2004 as a reissue year it is unsupported; if it should be 1966 it is simply wrong; evidence cannot decide.)

Entity is solid: MB search (sim 1.0) confirms Forever Love, Mark Whitfield, Verve (533 921-2), 1997-03-04; all 5 keyTracks present; albumDNA (jazz guitarist, Verve, 1997, ballads/standards, My One and Only Love, Nature Boy) all consistent. The one substantive claim that cannot be checked against pack evidence is 'vocalist Diana Krall guests on a few tracks' -- MB tracklist carries no personnel and the stored_url 'Thomas Whitfield (singer)' is a different gospel artist (wrong entity, not shipped). Absence is not contradiction; the Krall guesting claim is unverifiable from the evidence here (not refuted).

No MB match (release null, empty searchCandidates). The stored evidence wiki page is 'Grazing in the Grass' — the 1968 COMPOSITION/single page, about Hugh Masekela's recording, not the album under verification. It corroborates the artist (Hugh Masekela), the title track 'Grazing in the Grass', and 1968 — enough to confirm the right entity — but it does NOT confirm the album title 'Grazin'/Hugh Masekela's Grazin’', the Uni label, or the other keyTracks (Bajabula Bonke, etc.). Those album-level facts are unverifiable. No MB tracklist so keyTracks no_evidence. ours.wikipediaUrl null so none_stored.

Entity confirmed: MB search (sim 1.0) gives Leon Thomas in Berlin, Leon Thomas, releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 2000 (matched release is a 2002 BMG France reissue); all 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. Substantive claims that cannot be checked against pack evidence: (a) 'with the saxophonist/arranger Oliver Nelson' -- MB lists no personnel; (b) 'recorded in 1970 in Berlin' -- MB carries no recording date; (c) label 'Flying Dutchman' vs MB's reissue label 'BMG France' -- MB's 2002 reissue does not refute Flying Dutchman as the original label (absence is not contradiction). No contradictions, but multiple substantive personnel/date claims are unverifiable. opensearch returned no wiki page; ours.wikipediaUrl null.

MB embedded_mbid confirms artists Louis Armstrong + The Dukes of Dixieland, releaseGroup firstReleaseDate 1960, title match; all five keyTracks (Bourbon Street Parade/South/Washington and Lee Swing/Avalon/New Orleans) and the three DNA-named tunes present in MB tracklist. Right artist confirmed: the stored Abdullah Ibrahim wiki page is the correct entity and states he is 'known especially for Mannenberg, a jazz piece that became a notable anti-apartheid anthem.' But MB found no release, and the artist page does not document this album's label (Sun), exact year (1974), or tracklist. So the album-specific claims and the keyTracks (Mannenberg, Soweto Is Where It's At, Cape Town Fringe, The Pilgrim) cannot be verified against the available evidence. No contradiction; substantive facts unverifiable. ours.wikipediaUrl null.

Several substantive DNA claims cannot be checked against surviving evidence: Nat Adderley on cornet and Joe Zawinul on piano (no personnel in MB; wiki is wrong entity), the direct quote 'a sort of easy-going third horn,' and the pop-chart claim. No contradictions, but these uncheckable specifics drive an unverifiable verdict.

Entity confirmed: MB embedded_mbid gives Tenor Titans, Storyville Records (STCD 8288), 1997, credited Dexter Gordon + Ben Webster; all 5 keyTracks present. albumDNA names only Ben Webster (editorial; album is a Gordon/Webster split). Entity confidently correct: websearch (resolved=true) confirms pianist Bud Powell, ten-inch Blue Note BLP 5041, recorded at WOR Studios NYC on August 14, 1953, released 1954. year=1954 and label='Blue Note' verify; all five keyTracks match the websearch tracklist; 'Glass Enclosure', 'Autumn in New York' and 'I Want to Be Happy' are all on the tracklist. The only unverifiable substantive claim is the personnel 'George Duvivier on bass and Art Taylor on drums' -- the evidence packet does not list sidemen. Nothing contradicts it; verdict is unverifiable solely because that sideman claim cannot be checked against the available evidence. ours.wikipediaUrl matches the websearch-confirmed page.

Entity confidently correct: websearch (resolved=true; Wikipedia + AllMusic) confirms a studio album by jazz multi-instrumentalist Rahsaan/Roland Kirk on Mercury, recorded Aug 16-17 1961, released January 1962. year=1962 and label='Mercury' verify; all six keyTracks match the websearch tracklist; 'recorded August 1961', 'released by Mercury in January 1962', 'Blues for Alice' all verify. NOTE: the opensearch Wikipedia block mis-resolved to 'We Three Kings (disambiguation)' (a Christmas carol / unrelated albums) -- a wrong-entity opensearch hit -- but ours.wikipediaUrl is https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/We_Free_Kings, the correct page confirmed by websearch, so wikiLinkVerdict is correct. The only unverifiable substantive claim is the specific instrument list 'tenor saxophone, manzello, stritch, flute and siren, often several at once' -- the evidence says 'multi-instrumentalist' but does not enumerate the instruments. Nothing contradicts it; verdict is unverifiable solely because that instrumentation detail cannot be checked against the packet.

ours.wikipediaUrl is already null (correct). 'Belladonna' also does not appear in the provided websearch findings.tracklist (which is the Amaryllis side only: Night Shift, Anesthesia, Amaryllis, Side Effect, Hoodwink, 892 Teeth, plus Belladonna's 2nd track Moonburn); Belladonna is the suite name and may be a track title but is unconfirmed by pack evidence, so listed as unmatched. 'Amaryllis' and 'Night Shift' do match. Per spec, keyTracks were only repaired when null, so the stale 'The Gate' remains and is flagged here.

wrongClaims.problem: This 1997 Tudor album is a clarinet recital (performed by Karl‐Heinz Steffens et al.), not a Charlie Parker album. Parker appears only as composer of one track ('Yardbird Suite') and died in 1955; he cannot be the artist of a 1997 release.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: date 1997 ... artists: ... Honegger, Parker, Poulenc, Stravinsky, Karl‐Heinz Steffens, David Gazarov ... tracks: ... Yardbird Suite ...

regression.notes: albumDNA prose is actually CORRECT about the real album (a 1997 Tudor clarinet recital: Blue Rondo a la Turk / Debussy Premiere rhapsodie / Waltz for Debby / Clarinet Sonatina), and all 5 keyTracks match MB. The error is the artist FIELD = Charlie Parker (a composer credit pulled from the tracklist), not the prose. NOTE: this is the same MB release (MBID 505e34e7) as item 'blue-rondo-brubeck' -- two duplicate catalog entries of one clarinet-recital album mis-filed under two composer names. Repair: dedupe and set artist to the actual performer (Karl-Heinz Steffens).

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Kansas City 8: Get Together is a 1979 studio album by Count Basie.
